findings.title: North Star Metric: Daily Active Users Engaged in Collaboration
findings.description: The North Star metric for CollabDocs Real-time Editor is the Daily Active Users (DAU) engaged in collaboration sessions. This metric reflects the core value delivered by the product and aligns with the goal of enhancing real-time collaboration features.
assumptions: Increased collaboration features will drive higher DAU engagement.
recommendations: Monitor DAU closely post-launch of collaboration features.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of active users engaging in collaboration sessions.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Daily Collaboration Sessions
findings.description: This primary KPI measures the number of collaboration sessions initiated daily. It serves as a direct indicator of user engagement with the new real-time collaboration features.
assumptions: Users will initiate more collaboration sessions with improved features.
recommendations: Aim to increase daily collaboration sessions by 20% within the first quarter post-launch.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of collaboration sessions initiated.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Lock Frustrated Abandonment Rate
findings.description: This guardrail metric tracks the rate at which users abandon their editing sessions due to locking issues. It is crucial to ensure that this rate does not increase as new collaboration features are introduced.
assumptions: Improved collaboration features will reduce frustration and abandonment.
recommendations: Monitor this metric closely and aim to reduce it below 0.25 post-launch.

metadata.measurement_method: Daily tracking of abandonment rates due to locking issues.

findings.title: Measurement Gap: User Feedback on Collaboration Features
findings.description: There is insufficient data on user satisfaction and feedback specifically related to the new collaboration features. This gap needs to be addressed to ensure the features meet user needs.
assumptions: User feedback will provide insights into feature effectiveness.
recommendations: Implement a feedback mechanism post-launch to gather user insights.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: A/B Testing Collaboration Features
findings.description: Conduct A/B testing on different versions of the real-time collaboration features to determine which configurations yield the highest engagement and satisfaction.
assumptions: Different feature configurations will lead to varying user engagement levels.

evidence.excerpt: Enterprise customers asking for simultaneous editing

recommendations: Set up A/B tests with a minimum sample size of 1000 users per variant.

summary: The analysis identifies the North Star metric as DAU engaged in collaboration, with primary KPIs focusing on daily collaboration sessions and guardrails on abandonment rates. Gaps in user feedback and opportunities for A/B testing are highlighted to ensure successful feature implementation.
